mission.title: envoy: Allow loadable (dynamic) modules
mission.description: Envoy now supports dynamically loading shared library modules at runtime, enabling custom filters, loggers, and extensions without recompiling Envoy. This long-requested feature (11+ users) was implemented as an upstream extension based on the envoy-dynamic-modules project. Requires building shared libraries against the exact Envoy source tree version.

steps.title: Verify Envoy version supports dynamic modules
steps.description:
Dynamic modules support was added starting with the upstreaming effort in Envoy 1.32+. Check your version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -l app=envoy --all-namespaces
envoy --version 2>/dev/null || kubectl exec deploy/envoy -- envoy --version
```
Note: Envoy is commonly deployed via Istio or Envoy Gateway — check the sidecar or gateway pod version.

steps.title: Build a dynamic module
steps.description:
Dynamic modules are C/C++/Rust shared libraries (.so files) compiled against the Envoy source tree. The module SDK is at https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/tree/main/source/extensions/dynamic_modules.

Example build using the Envoy dynamic modules SDK:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy.git
cd envoy
bazel build //source/extensions/dynamic_modules/sdk/...
```
For a simpler starting point, see the reference implementation at https://github.com/mathetake/envoy-dynamic-modules.

steps.title: Configure Envoy to load the module
steps.description:
Add the dynamic module to your Envoy configuration. The module is loaded via the `dynamic_modules` extension:
```yaml
static_resources:
  listeners:
    - name: listener_0
      filter_chains:
        - filters:
            - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
              typed_config:
                http_filters:
                  - name: envoy.filters.http.dynamic_modules
                    typed_config:
                      "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.dynamic_modules.v3.DynamicModuleFilter
                      dynamic_module_config:
                        name: my_module
                        file_path: /etc/envoy/modules/my_module.so
```

steps.title: Verify the module is loaded
steps.description:
Check Envoy logs for successful module loading:
```bash
kubectl logs deploy/envoy --tail=50 | grep -i 'dynamic_module\|module.*loaded'
```
You should see log entries confirming the module was loaded successfully.

resolution.summary: Envoy supports dynamic loading of shared library modules via the dynamic_modules extension, upstreamed from the envoy-dynamic-modules project (https://github.com/mathetake/envoy-dynamic-modules). Modules are C/C++/Rust shared libraries built against the Envoy source tree. The initial implementation was added in PR #35550.

prerequisites.description: A running Envoy deployment (standalone, Istio sidecar, or Envoy Gateway). Building custom modules requires Bazel and the Envoy source tree.
